findings.title: Command Injection via exec() Template
findings.description: Shell command with interpolated values enables command injection. Use execFile() with argument arrays.
findings.fix: Use execFile(cmd, [arg1, arg2]) instead of exec(`cmd ${arg}`)

findings.title: Command Injection via exec() Template
findings.description: Shell command with interpolated values enables command injection. Use execFile() with argument arrays.
findings.fix: Use execFile(cmd, [arg1, arg2]) instead of exec(`cmd ${arg}`)

findings.title: SQL Injection via Template Literal
findings.description: SQL query with interpolated template variable. Use parameterized queries ($1, ?) or an ORM.
findings.fix: Use parameterized queries: db.query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1", [userId])

findings.title: Raw SQL Query (Prisma/Sequelize/Knex)
findings.description: ORM raw query with interpolated values bypasses parameterization. Use tagged templates or bind parameters.
findings.fix: Prisma: use $queryRaw`...${Prisma.sql}...`; Sequelize: use bind parameters

findings.title: Weak Password Hashing (MD5/SHA)
findings.description: MD5/SHA are not suitable for password hashing. Use bcrypt, scrypt, or argon2.
findings.fix: Use bcrypt.hash(password, 12) or argon2.hash(password)

findings.title: SSRF: User Input in fetch()
findings.description: User-controlled URL passed to fetch() enables SSRF. Validate against an allowlist.
findings.fix: Validate URL against allowlist: new URL(input).hostname must match allowed hosts

findings.title: Code Injection: eval() Usage
findings.description: eval() executes arbitrary code. Replace with JSON.parse(), Function, or a safer alternative.
findings.fix: Replace eval() with JSON.parse() or a domain-specific parser

findings.title: Code Injection via new Function()
findings.description: new Function() is equivalent to eval(). Avoid dynamic code generation.
findings.fix: Refactor to avoid dynamic code generation

findings.title: ReDoS: Nested Quantifiers in Regex
findings.description: Regex with nested quantifiers like (a+)+ or (\w+)* causes catastrophic backtracking.
findings.fix: Rewrite to avoid nested repetition or use a non-backtracking engine (re2 package).

findings.title: XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML
findings.description: dangerouslySetInnerHTML can introduce XSS if the value contains user input.
findings.fix: Sanitize with DOMPurify: dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{__html: DOMPurify.sanitize(html)}}

findings.title: CSRF Protection Disabled
findings.description: CSRF protection is explicitly disabled. State-changing requests need CSRF tokens.
findings.fix: Enable CSRF protection or use SameSite=Strict cookies

findings.title: TLS rejectUnauthorized: false
findings.description: rejectUnauthorized: false disables TLS certificate checking.
findings.fix: Remove rejectUnauthorized: false, or use a proper CA bundle

findings.title: Weak Cryptography: ECB Mode
findings.description: ECB mode leaks patterns in ciphertext. Use CBC or GCM mode.
findings.fix: Use AES-256-GCM: createCipheriv("aes-256-gcm", key, iv)

findings.title: Possible Typosquat: "bcryptjs" (similar to "bcrypt")
findings.description: Package "bcryptjs" is 2 character(s) away from popular package "bcrypt". This could be a typosquatting attempt.
findings.fix: Verify this is the intended package. Did you mean "bcrypt"?

findings.title: Scoped Packages Without Registry Pin: @better-auth, @dnd-kit, @hookform, @phosphor-icons, @react-three, @tabler, @faker-js, @playwright, @tailwindcss, @vitejs, @vitest
findings.description: Scoped packages (@better-auth, @dnd-kit, @hookform, @phosphor-icons, @react-three, @tabler, @faker-js, @playwright, @tailwindcss, @vitejs, @vitest) found without a .npmrc pinning the scope to a private registry. An attacker could claim the scope on the public npm registry.
findings.fix: Add to .npmrc: @yourscope:registry=https://your-private-registry.com/

findings.title: Dockerfile: No Non-Root USER
findings.description: No USER instruction found. Container runs as root, enabling escape attacks.
findings.fix:
Add before CMD: RUN addgroup -S app && adduser -S app -G app
USER app
